LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT MICHELLE TORRES' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, IS DENIED. PER THE DECLARATION OF DR. SLAUGHTER, THERE ARE TRIABLE DISPUTES WHETHER DR. TORRES BREACHED THE STANDARD OF CARE AND WHETHER THAT BREACH WAS A SUBSTANTIAL FACTOR IN CAUSING ANY OF PLAINTIFF MICHAEL SEONG'S CLAIMED INJURIES. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: REYES F. HUNTER, CSR NO. 6576, E-MAIL: RHUNTER@SFTC.ORG (L&M302)

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT MICHELLE TORRES' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Per the declaration of Dr. Slaughter, there are triable disputes whether Dr. Torres breached the standard of care and whether that breach was a substantial factor in causing any of plaintiff Michael Seong's claimed injuries. Written order is signed and filed in open court.
